sec.title: 8.1 In-line code
sec.p: Based on data up to and including 1971, eruptions on La Palma happen every 79.8 years on average.

sec.title: 8.2 Using citations
sec.p: Studies of the magma systems feeding the volcano, such as Marrero et al. (2019), have proposed that there are two main magma reservoirs feeding the Cumbre Vieja volcano; one in the mantle (30-40km depth) which charges and in turn feeds a shallower crustal reservoir (10-20km depth).

sec.title: 8.3 Figure References
sec.p: Eight eruptions have been recorded since the late 1400s (Figure 1).

caption.p: Figure 1: Timeline of recent earthquakes on La Palma

sec.title: 8.5 LATEX Math
sec.p:
Let x x denote the number of eruptions in a year. Then,
x x can be modeled by a Poisson distribution

where \lambda λ is the rate of eruptions per year. Using Equation 1, the probability of an eruption in the next
t t years can be calculated.

sec.title: 8.6 Markdown Table

caption.p: Table 1: Recent historic eruptions on La Palma

sec.p: Table 1 summarises the eruptions recorded since the colonization of the islands by Europeans in the late 1400s.

sec.title: 8.8 Referencing Figures
sec.p: La Palma is one of the west most islands in the Volcanic Archipelago of the Canary Islands (Figure 2).

sec.title: 8.9 Using figure from embeded notbook

caption.p: Figure 3: Locations of earthquakes on La Palma since 2017

sec.p: Figure 3 shows the location of recent Earthquakes on La Palma.

element-citation.article-title: Strategies for the development of volcanic hazard maps in monogenetic volcanic fields: The example of La Palma (Canary Islands)

sec.code: eruptions <- c(1492, 1585, 1646, 1677, 1712, 1949, 1971, 2021) n_eruptions <- length(eruptions)
avg_years_between_eruptions <- mean(diff(eruptions[-n_eruptions])) avg_years_between_eruptions

sec.title: 8.1 In-line code
sec.p: Based on data up to and including 1971, eruptions on La Palma happen every 79.8 years on average.

sec.title: 8.2 Using citations
sec.p: Studies of the magma systems feeding the volcano, such as Marrero et al. (2019), have proposed that there are two main magma reservoirs feeding the Cumbre Vieja volcano; one in the mantle (30-40km depth) which charges and in turn feeds a shallower crustal reservoir (10-20km depth).

sec.title: 8.3 Figure References
sec.p: Eight eruptions have been recorded since the late 1400s (Figure 1).

sec.code: par(mar = c(3, 1, 1, 1) + 0.1) plot(eruptions, rep(0, n_eruptions), pch = "|", axes = FALSE) axis(1) box()

caption.p: Figure 1: Timeline of recent earthquakes on La Palma

sec.title: 8.5 LATEX Math
sec.p:
Let x x denote the number of eruptions in a year. Then,
x x can be modeled by a Poisson distribution

where \lambda λ is the rate of eruptions per year. Using Equation 1, the probability of an eruption in the next
t t years can be calculated.

sec.title: 8.6 Markdown Table

caption.p: Table 1: Recent historic eruptions on La Palma

sec.p: Table 1 summarises the eruptions recorded since the colonization of the islands by Europeans in the late 1400s.

sec.title: 8.8 Referencing Figures
sec.p: La Palma is one of the west most islands in the Volcanic Archipelago of the Canary Islands (Figure 2).

sec.title: 8.9 Using figure from embeded notbook

sec.code: la_palma |> ggplot(aes(Longitude, Latitude)) + geom_point(aes(color = Magnitude, size = 40-`Depth(km)`)) + scale_color_viridis_c(direction = -1) + scale_size(range = c(0.5, 2), guide = "none") + theme_bw()

caption.p: Figure 3: Locations of earthquakes on La Palma since 2017

sec.p: Figure 3 shows the location of recent Earthquakes on La Palma.

element-citation.article-title: Strategies for the development of volcanic hazard maps in monogenetic volcanic fields: The example of La Palma (Canary Islands)

sec.code: library(tidyverse)

boxed-text.preformat: Warning: package 'tibble' was built under R version 4.5.2
Warning: package 'tidyr' was built under R version 4.5.2
Warning: package 'purrr' was built under R version 4.5.2
Warning: package 'dplyr' was built under R version 4.5.2

── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ── ✔ dplyr 1.2.0 ✔ readr 2.1.5 ✔ forcats 1.0.0 ✔ stringr 1.6.0 ✔ ggplot2 3.5.2 ✔ tibble 3.3.1 ✔ lubridate 1.9.4 ✔ tidyr 1.3.2 ✔ purrr 1.2.1 ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ── ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter() ✖ dplyr::lag() masks stats::lag() ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors

body.p: Read a clean version of data:

sec.code: la_palma <- read_csv("../1_data/la-palma.csv")

boxed-text.preformat:
Rows: 11347 Columns: 5 ── Column specification ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Delimiter: "," dbl (4): Longitude, Latitude, Depth(km), Magnitude dttm (1): DateTime
ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data. ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.

sec.code: #| against longitude." la_palma |> ggplot(aes(Longitude, Latitude)) + geom_point(aes(color = Magnitude, size = 40-`Depth(km)`)) + scale_color_viridis_c(direction = -1) + scale_size(range = c(0.5, 2), guide = "none") + theme_bw()

caption.p: Locations of earthquakes on La Palma since 2017

sec.code: library(tidyverse)

boxed-text.preformat: Warning: package 'tibble' was built under R version 4.5.2
Warning: package 'tidyr' was built under R version 4.5.2
Warning: package 'purrr' was built under R version 4.5.2
Warning: package 'dplyr' was built under R version 4.5.2

── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ── ✔ dplyr 1.2.0 ✔ readr 2.1.5 ✔ forcats 1.0.0 ✔ stringr 1.6.0 ✔ ggplot2 3.5.2 ✔ tibble 3.3.1 ✔ lubridate 1.9.4 ✔ tidyr 1.3.2 ✔ purrr 1.2.1 ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ── ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter() ✖ dplyr::lag() masks stats::lag() ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors

sec.code: library(sf)

boxed-text.preformat: Warning: package 'sf' was built under R version 4.5.2
Linking to GEOS 3.13.0, GDAL 3.8.5, PROJ 9.5.1; sf_use_s2() is TRUE

sec.code: library(ggspatial) source("../R/functions.R")

body.p: I made a function to combine the data, but they are too big for github. So I put folder they are saved to in the gitignore.

sec.code:
#### COMBINE HSIS DATA #### # run this once to combined the files
# The folder with the hsis data you want to combine # hsis_folder <- "../1_data/HSIS Charlotte Data/" # # This functions combines the appropriate hsis files if you have multiple years # combine_HSIS_files(hsis_folder)

#### PULL IN AND CLEAN DATA #### combined_hsis_folder <- "../1_data/HSIS Charlotte Data/combined_data/"
busstop_data <- read_csv(paste0(combined_hsis_folder, "/","combined_18_to_24_catsStops.csv"))

boxed-text.preformat: New names: Rows: 20994 Columns: 12 ── Column specification ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Delimiter: "," chr (8): Bench, Shelter, routes, Direction, Pole_Locat, Sidewalk, Trans_P, o... dbl (4): ...1, StopID, x, y ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data. ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message. • `` -> `...1`

sec.code: busstop_data <- busstop_data[,2:ncol(busstop_data)] crash_data <- read_csv(paste0(combined_hsis_folder, "/","combined_18_to_24_crash.csv"))

boxed-text.preformat: New names: Rows: 265157 Columns: 22 ── Column specification ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Delimiter: "," chr (13): DATE_VAL, CRASH_TYPE, LIT, WTHR, PRIMARY, SECONDARY, URBAN_RU, RD_... dbl (9): ...1, CRSH_ID, DATE_VAL_Y, MILT_TIME, CRSH_LEVL, LATITUDE, LONGITU... ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data. ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message. • `` -> `...1`

sec.code: crash_data <- crash_data[,2:ncol(crash_data)]

body.p: Making a data frame that will give true for each column that had no changes throughout the year

sec.code: stop_changes <- busstop_data |> group_by(StopID) |> summarise(x_unq = length(unique(x)) == 1, y_unq = length(unique(y)) == 1, Bench_unq = length(unique(Bench)) == 1, Shelter_unq = length(unique(Shelter)) == 1, routes_unq = length(unique(routes)) == 1, Direction_unq = length(unique(Direction)) == 1, Pole_Locat_unq = length(unique(Pole_Locat)) == 1, Sidewalk_unq = length(unique(Sidewalk)) == 1, Trans_P_unq = length(unique(Trans_P)) == 1, orgnl_file = length(unique(orgnl_file)) == 1)

body.p: it looks like there have been some changes throughout the year

sec.code: colSums(stop_changes)

boxed-text.preformat: StopID x_unq y_unq Bench_unq Shelter_unq 98245785 147 147 3136 3134 routes_unq Direction_unq Pole_Locat_unq Sidewalk_unq Trans_P_unq 2770 3148 3148 3149 3150 orgnl_file 57

body.p: looking at the unique stop ids

sec.code:
stop_id_count <- busstop_data |> group_by(StopID) |> summarise(count = n(), file = paste(unique(orgnl_file), collapse = ", "))
stop_id_count |> filter(count == 2) |> select(file) |> unique()

boxed-text.preformat: # A tibble: 3 × 1 file <chr> 1 clt19catsStops.csv, clt20catsStops.csv 2 clt18catsStops.csv, clt19catsStops.csv 3 clt19catsStops.csv, clt22catsStops.csv

sec.code: busstop_data |> filter(orgnl_file == "clt24catsStops.csv") |> arrange(StopID)

boxed-text.preformat: # A tibble: 2,992 × 11 StopID x y Bench Shelter routes Direction Pole_Locat Sidewalk <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <chr> <chr> <chr> <chr> <chr> <chr> 1 40 1453640. 543460. <NA> <NA> 4 inbound rear faci… sidewalk 2 120 1424129. 535050. <NA> <NA> 235, 60 outbound forward f… sidewalk 3 126 1423809. 536203. <NA> <NA> 235, 60 inbound forward f… sidewalk 4 127 1423858. 536575. <NA> <NA> 235, 60 outbound forward f… sidewalk 5 140 1424115. 535018. <NA> <NA> 235, 60 inbound forward f… <NA> 6 160 1477221. 534317. Bench <NA> 221, 9 inbound forward f… sidewalk 7 170 1479217. 533501. <NA> Shelter 221, 40x… inbound forward f… sidewalk 8 180 1479323. 533416. <NA> Shelter 221, 40x… outbound forward f… sidewalk 9 183 1471247. 573835. <NA> <NA> 50 not appl… other sidewalk 10 200 1460865. 561616. <NA> <NA> 22 inbound forward f… <NA> # ℹ 2,982 more rows # ℹ 2 more variables: Trans_P <chr>, orgnl_file <chr>

body.p: When I plot lon lat points as is, they don’t line up.

sec.code: busstop_data |> arrange(StopID) |> ggplot() + geom_point(aes(x = x, y = y, color = orgnl_file))

body.p: I noticed that some files have an extra digit at the end, so I selected all the files with common lat lon formats and made two plots.

sec.code: busstop_data |> filter(orgnl_file %in% c("clt18catsStops.csv", "clt19catsStops.csv", "clt20catsStops.csv", "clt22catsStops.csv")) |> ggplot() + geom_point(aes(x = x, y = y, color = orgnl_file))
busstop_data |> filter(orgnl_file %in% c("clt23catsStops.csv", "clt24catsStops.csv", "clt21catsStops.csv")) |> ggplot() + geom_point(aes(x = x, y = y, color = orgnl_file))

body.p: testing to see if I can get all stops on the same map
small: These datasets have 5 or 6 digits in the coordinates

sec.code: small_coords_stops <- busstop_data |> filter(orgnl_file %in% c("clt18catsStops.csv", "clt19catsStops.csv", "clt20catsStops.csv", "clt22catsStops.csv")) |> mutate(x = x*10, y = y*10)

body.p: big: These datasets have 6 or 7 digits in the coordinates

sec.code: big_coords_stops <- busstop_data |> filter(orgnl_file %in% c("clt23catsStops.csv", "clt24catsStops.csv", "clt21catsStops.csv"))

body.p: this df has combined the data after adjusting the coordinates

sec.code: bound_stops <- bind_rows(small_coords_stops, big_coords_stops) |> mutate(lon = x / 10000, lat = y / 10000)

body.p: Now I just need to find out how to get the coordinates as an sf object to plot on the map correctly. I am not done with this code chunk yet. I think I am doing something wrong with the crs.

sec.code:
df_sf <- st_as_sf(bound_stops, coords = c("lat", "lon"), crs = 4326)
# 1. Turn coordinates into sf object (assuming CRS 32610 - UTM Zone 10N) df_sf <- st_as_sf(bound_stops, coords = c("x", "y"), crs = 26917) # 2. Transform to Lat/Long (WGS84, EPSG:4326) df_longlat <- st_transform(df_sf, crs = 4326)
ggplot(df_sf) + annotation_map_tile() + geom_sf(aes(color = orgnl_file))

boxed-text.preformat: Loading required namespace: raster
Zoom: 7
